perturbation.original_text: With respect to any oral or visual communication or other intangible information which a Disclosing Party would like to be treated as Confidential Information under this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall notify Recipient of such fact at the time of disclosure and within fiftee n (15) days thereafter, Disclosing Party shall send Recipient a written memorandum outlining the information deemed to be Confidential Information. Such memorandum shall be marked “Confidential.”
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to any oral or visual communication or other intangible information which a Disclosing Party would like to be treated as Confidential Information under this Agreement, the Disclosing Party *might* notify Recipient of such fact at the time of disclosure and within fiftee n (15) days thereafter, Disclosing Party *might* send Recipient a written memorandum outlining the information deemed to be Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'shall' with 'might', the obligation to notify the recipient and send a written memorandum for oral, visual, or intangible information is now optional. This creates a contradiction because Section 8 relies on a clear identification process for Confidential Information to enforce injunctive relief. The term 'might' makes it uncertain whether the information has to be shared or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is a unique and valuable asset of Disclosing Party, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result i n irreparable injury to Disclosing Party for which monetary damages alone would not be an Non-disclosure Agreement – v. 1.0 3 adequate remedy. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality and/or of this agreement, the Disclosing Party sh all be entitled to seek an injunction prohibiting any such breach or to specific performance or other equitable relief as a remedy available to Disclosing Party. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'shall,' making the notification and memorandum mandatory for confidential information. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'might,' making it optional. This contradicts the contradicted text because the contradicted text allows the Disclosing Party to seek an injunction prohibiting any such breach based on the Confidential Information. If the Disclosing Party uses 'might' to notify, then there is no longer a guarantee that the Confidential Information was properly identified. As a result, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain in the property of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain in the property of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(including without limitation information in computer software or held in electronic storage media)' the clause becomes less encompassing. The in-text contradiction arises as Section 12 touches on export control laws for institution of higher learning, but with the current change the clause is not including information regarding computer software, the two parties, can easily fall into problems, and there would be no explicit declaration on the subject matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that it will not directly or indirectly transmit, by way of trans - shipment, export, diversion or otherwise, any Confidential Information of the other Party except in accordance with any and all applicable United States expor t control laws and regulations. As an institution of higher learning, Carnegie Mellon generally performs fundamental research that is exempt from export control licensing under applicable export control laws. As a result, Carnegie Mellon typically does not wish to take receipt of export -controlled information, except as may be specifically agreed to by Carnegie Mellon and for which Carnegie Mellon has made specific arrangements. __________________ agrees that it will not provide or make accessible to Carnegie Mellon any export -controlled Confidential Information without first informing the Carnegie Mellon’s Office of Sponsored Programs of the export -controlled nature of the Confidential Information and obtaining from Carnegie Mellon’s Office of Sponsored Programs its written consent to accept such information as well as any specific instructions regarding the mechanism pursuant to which such information should be passed.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly included 'information in computer software or held in electronic storage media' as part of the Confidential Information. The changed text removes this explicit inclusion, making the scope of Confidential Information less clear. This creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which discusses export control laws and regulations, particularly regarding institutions of higher learning like Carnegie Mellon. Because the changed text no longer explicitly includes computer software, it weakens the enforceability of the agreement regarding the transfer of such information, which is crucial to the export control considerations mentioned in the contradicted section. This omission creates a strong contradiction because it undermines the ability to adequately control export-controlled information, which is a primary concern of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, unless it is terminated earlier by either party providing the other party with at least thirty (30) days prior written notice, or unless it is extended as mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, unless it is extended as mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'unless it is terminated earlier by either party providing the other party with at least thirty (30) days prior written notice', the possibility of early termination is omitted. This creates a contradiction as Section 8 is about Injunctive Relief, as such, one of the scenario might be that parties will terminate the contract early, as such, with this new change, this cannot be possible. By omitting this clause it creates uncertainty about both parties and their obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is a unique and valuable asset of Disclosing Party, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result i n irreparable injury to Disclosing Party for which monetary damages alone would not be an Non-disclosure Agreement – v. 1.0 3 adequate remedy. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality and/or of this agreement, the Disclosing Party sh all be entitled to seek an injunction prohibiting any such breach or to specific performance or other equitable relief as a remedy available to Disclosing Party. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for early termination with 30 days' notice. The changed text removes this possibility, making the agreement term fixed for one year unless extended. Section 8 allows for injunctive relief in the event of a breach of the agreement. By removing the possibility of early termination, the changed text contradicts Section 8, because it restricts the circumstances under which the agreement can be breached and thus, requiring injunctive relief. The omission directly impacts the enforceability and scope of Section 8, creating a contradiction.
